channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-1285] Test failure on test suite sanity: get_stripe_info: command not found
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for nasf <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/e34eab8c-7d85-11e1-aaf6-525400d2bfa6.

get_stripe_info is removed by the patch: http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,2022
But it is still used by some test scripts, like sanity, then caused following failures:

/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5276: get_stripe_info: command not found
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5277: [: -ne: unary operator expected
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5280: [: -ne: unary operator expected
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5283: [: -ne: unary operator expected
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5276: get_stripe_info: command not found
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5277: [: -ne: unary operator expected
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5280: [: -ne: unary operator expected
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh: line 5283: [: -ne: unary operator expected
sanity test_102d: @@@@@@ FAIL: all files are being extracted starting from OST index 0
Dumping lctl log to /logdir/test_logs/2012-04-02/lustre-reviews-el6-x86_64_4621_-7f61d93d5c60/sanity.test_102d.*.1333429553.log
item.summary: Test failure on test suite sanity: get_stripe_info: command not found

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

Integrated in lustre-master » i686,server,el5,ofa #484
LU-1285 tests: fix tar breakage in 102d/102f/102j (Revision 8d0bf30d5dc700b176c9e66a103e8c31fff20d47)

Files :

  • lustre/tests/replay-ost-single.sh

Integrated in lustre-master » x86_64,client,sles11,inkernel #484
LU-1285 tests: fix tar breakage in 102d/102f/102j (Revision 8d0bf30d5dc700b176c9e66a103e8c31fff20d47)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : 8d0bf30d5dc700b176c9e66a103e8c31fff20d47
Files :

  • lustre/tests/sanity.sh
  • lustre/tests/replay-ost-single.sh
Fix landed for 2.3

It turns out that the reason these tests did not fail in my local testing (FC13) or during review was due to the lack of a lustre-aware tar being installed. The Lustre fixes were only added into FC14 and RHEL6. Once I installed the RHEL6 tar (tar-1.23-3.el6.x86_64) the tests were run and indeed were failing.

http://review.whamcloud.com/2458

Those tests all reported success when run previously, so this breakage wasn't noticed. Sadly, the test runs that were done for the patch landing (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/9151bc76-6eed-11e1-b737-5254004bbbd3, https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/ebf3704e-5f9c-11e1-9458-5254004bbbd3) 102d, 102f, 102j report PASS (not SKIP or FAIL) and do not have logs, so I cannot see whether they were run incorrectly or there is a bug in the test framework.

I'll submit a patch to fix this.
